codeReview.summary: This is a verification-only gate feature — no code changes were made. The worker correctly identified this as a gate and skipped file reading, audit checklist, and TDD per the kernel-auditor skill's 'Gate/verification features' special case. Independent verification confirms all assertions hold: (1) cargo build --release succeeds with zero errors/warnings, (2) full test suite passes (1006 tests, 0 failures across 24 test binaries), (3) all 33 kernel .rs files have AUDIT comments with unsafe block enumeration, (4) all 3 critical one-line fixes from DESIGN.md section 11 are intact. The git diff from 8441e45..364c412 (M3/M4 audit commits) shows only AUDIT comment additions — no function signatures, logic, or API changes were made, trivially satisfying VAL-CROSS-005. The commit 364c412 belongs to the preceding audit-paging-mmio feature; this gate correctly produced no new commit.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The AUDIT comments across the 33 kernel files use slightly inconsistent formats for counting unsafe sites. Some count 'unsafe blocks' only (e.g., memory_mapped_io.rs: '3 unsafe blocks'), while others count unsafe blocks + unsafe fn + unsafe impl separately (e.g., heap.rs: '2 unsafe blocks, 3 unsafe fn, 2 unsafe impl'). The kernel-auditor SKILL.md example uses 'N unsafe blocks verified' as the template. AGENTS.md does not specify the expected AUDIT comment format for files with unsafe fn/impl in addition to unsafe blocks. This inconsistency is non-blocking but could cause confusion in future audits about whether a file was fully audited.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: heap.rs line 1: '2 unsafe blocks, 3 unsafe fn, 2 unsafe impl'; slab.rs line 1: '2 unsafe blocks + 2 unsafe fn'; memory_mapped_io.rs line 1: '3 unsafe blocks verified'. SKILL.md example: 'N unsafe blocks verified'.

summary: Gate feature passes all checks. Independent verification confirms: build clean, 1006 tests pass, all 33 .rs files have AUDIT comments, all critical one-line fixes (emergency stack sizing .space 4096*8 matching MAX_CORES=8, .bss.exc_stacks ALIGN(4096), virtio push_chain desc.next=next_free) are intact. M3/M4 changes were purely AUDIT comment additions with no API or logic changes. Worker followed the kernel-auditor skill procedure correctly, using the gate/verification shortcut path.
